name: ui-ux
description: "Use when improving interface clarity, hierarchy, usability, visual consistency, interaction feedback, and role-based experience differences. Keywords: ui, ux, design, usability, layout, accessibility, copy, user flow."
---

# UI UX Skill

## Goal
Improve usability and visual communication so users can complete tasks faster with fewer mistakes.

## Use When
- Refining page structure, labels, and interaction cues.
- Improving role-based clarity in a shared UI shell.
- Enhancing visual hierarchy and consistency.
- Preparing presentation-ready screens.

## Workflow
1. Identify primary task per screen.
2. Reduce cognitive load: clear labels, grouping, and feedback.
3. Differentiate states and permissions visually.
4. Improve microcopy for guidance and errors.
5. Check mobile and desktop readability.
6. Preserve existing design language unless intentionally evolving it.

## Quality Checklist
- Important actions are obvious.
- Read-only vs editable states are unambiguous.
- Empty/error/success states are informative.
- Contrast and spacing support readability.
- Flow feels consistent across modules.

## Output Format
- Before vs after UX intent.
- Specific UI decisions and rationale.
- Role-based experience differences.
- Quick usability test scenarios.
